media.name: Crude cashew apple juice (CAJ), no aeration (PMC3958174)
media.base_medium: Crude cashew apple juice (CAJ) from Anacardium occidentale L. (natural fruit juice used undiluted as sole cultivation medium, no aeration)
media.base_medium_reference: Oliveira AH, Ogrodowski CC, de Macedo AC, Santana MHA, Goncalves LRB. Cashew apple juice as microbial cultivation medium for non-immunogenic hyaluronic acid production. Braz J Microbiol. 2014;44(4):1097-1104. PMID: 24688498; PMCID: PMC3958174. Composition further attributed by the authors to Rocha MVP et al. Production of biosurfactant by Pseudomonas aeruginosa grown on cashew apple juice. Appl Biochem Biotechnol. 2007;136:185-194. doi:10.1007/s12010-007-9050-6
media.verification_evidence: Paper (PMC3958174): crude/pure CAJ used without supplementation. CAJ contains '10% of reducing sugars (glucose and fructose), phosphorous and amino acids' plus 'B-vitamins, particularly thiamine, riboflavin, niacin and folic acid', total nitrogen '0.04% w/v'. The 'no aeration' record corresponds to the anaerobic Erlenmeyer-flask condition (37C, 150 rpm); the paper also ran an aerobic (2 vvm) CAJ f
media.citation: Medium composition from PMC3958174 (see GrowthDB record).
